element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player learns to swap and arrange blocks in rows before rising pressure begins
fundamental clearing mechanic, practiced with slow rise at start
players discover blocks above cleared lines fall and create combos; core depth mechanic
blocks steadily rise toward top of playfield; speed increases over time, creating escalating tension
failure state becomes increasingly likely as stack height approaches boundary

typical_relief_triggers: creating chain reactions that drop multiple lines
typical_tension_triggers: blocks rising steadily toward top of playfield
speed increase over time in Endless mode
new blocks generating from bottom without player control
narrow gaps between block height and failure boundary
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating pressure puzzle; tension builds through unavoidable rising blocks; relief comes from player-initiated clearing actions and cascades. Speed ramps continuously, shifting from exploratory play to reactive survival.

difficulty_curve.early_game: gentle, slow-rising blocks allow learning control and basic matching before speed increases
difficulty_curve.mid_game: rising speed accelerates; players must balance clearing blocks efficiently while managing stack height; chain reaction depth becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: continuous acceleration in block rise; reactive play dominates; stack approaches boundary frequently
notable_spikes: speed ramps in Endless mode create progressive difficulty wall
Stage Clear mode forces puzzle-solving under move constraints
Story Mode opponents likely increase in clearing speed/efficiency

notable_level_patterns.description: series of computer-controlled opponents pits player against escalating AI efficiency; backgrounds change between opponents (Windy, Lakitu, etc.); serves as narrative progression structure
blocks rise steadily; speed increases continuously over time; no end condition except failure; pure skill-based attrition. Background changes reflect progression depth (Windy->Lakitu themes).
player must clear all blocks below a marked boundary line; blocks do not auto-rise; puzzle-like fixed-state challenges; progression through stages adds new arrangement complexities
preset block arrangement; player must clear all blocks within set number of moves; no auto-rise; pure puzzle logic without time/speed pressure; optimal-play optimization
two-minute time limit; maximize points via clearing and combos rather than survival; rewards efficient cascades and multi-block clears

sources_used: Wikipedia: Tetris Attack / Panel de Pon article (Gameplay section, Development section)
